title: symphyotrichum × gravesii
intro: Symphyotrichum × gravesii, also known as Graves' Aster, is a sterile hybrid plant of the Aster family, resulting from the cross between Symphyotrichum novi-belgii and Symphyotrichum puniceum. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which are attributed to its essential oils and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Symphyotrichum × gravesii include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and ease respiratory issues, such as congestion and coughs. The herbal preparations of Symphyotrichum × gravesii typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
